channel.title: Health
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from Health, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Exercise Benefits for a Faster Metabolism
item.description: More intense workouts can temporarily raise your metabolic rate. Learn how exercise affects metabolism and which workouts have the greatest impact.

item.title: How HIV Affects Older Adults Differently
Reasons Why Women Experience Hair Thinning
This Is The Most Consistent Predictor Of Longevity — Are You Doing It?
item.description: Plus, check out these mobility-focused exercises to do at every age

item.title: I Asked a Fitness Expert About Vibration Plates — Here's What She Really Thinks
item.description: The truth about vibration plates from someone who's actually read the research

item.title: This Is Why Coffee Is Linked To Improved Longevity & Heart Health
item.description: Coffee has hundreds of bioactive ingredients. These are 7 of the key antioxidants.

item.title: Your Air Fryer Might Be Working Against You—Here's the Easy Fix
item.description: It could be doing more harm than good if you're not staying on top of frequent cleanings.

item.title: This Metric Is The #1 Predictor Of Future Strength — Here’s How To Test Yours
item.description: This “vital sign” doesn’t require a doctor’s office

item.title: This complex brain network may explain many of Parkinson's stranger symptoms
item.description: Parkinson's disease appears to disrupt a brain network involved in everything from movement to memory.

item.title: Hospitals are posting prices for patients. It's mostly industry using the data
item.description: The Trump administration pushed for price transparency in health care. But instead of patients shopping for services, it's mostly health systems and i...

item.title: In a world built for sitting, here's how to stay active — even when stuck inside
item.description: In the office, classroom and living room, working and relaxing mean sitting still. Our bodies evolved without chairs. Here are some tips for getting o...

item.title: Olympic COVID restrictions are gone, but some athletes are still self-quarantining
item.description: For most people, the pandemic days of masking are behind them. In certain corners of the Winter Olympics, though, things still look a lot like they di...

item.title: Modest 'mental exercise' can reduce risk of dementia for decades, study finds
item.description: One type of cognitive training appears to reduce the risk of dementia 20 years later.
